phonetic: 藢su藧p蓹p蓹藞z瑟蕛n; 藢sju藧p蓹p蓹藞z瑟蕛n
examples: In this example, we exploited the quantum mechanics principle of superposition.
What appears to the naked eye to be glowing is actually a superposition of different lines of distinct frequency. So, this is quantized.
Freestanding kivas discovered in the ruins of European-style missionary communities have been explained by some historians as examples of "superposition".
Superposition is, nevertheless, crucial to the idea of building what is known as a quantum computer.
But the computer would work only if the operator could interact with it without destroying the superposition.
